item.title: Version 20.1
item.description:
A big stability and polish release — the headline fix is the iPhone playback freeze on the Native engine, plus a major overhaul of resume, media‑server reliability, the guide, and loading indicators.

Playback

  • Fixed the periodic video freeze on iPhone — live channels on the Native engine could freeze for ~a second every few minutes while audio kept playing. The engine also now auto‑recovers when a server drops the connection.
  • The Native engine works again over plain‑HTTP on iOS 26, restoring the higher‑quality deinterlacer for sports and interlaced channels.
  • Sheds expensive image processing when the device gets hot, so long sessions stutter less.
  • Assorted player fixes (volume‑slider drag, catch‑up timing, engine selection).

Watch progress & resume

  • Resume actually works now across movies and series, on IPTV and Emby/Plex/Jellyfin — progress is saved reliably during playback.
  • Continue Watching is sorted by most‑recent; “Start from Beginning” starts immediately.
  • Fixed progress routing with multiple media servers; Plex now reports mid‑playback.

Media servers & artwork

  • Fixed Jellyfin opening the Emby view, and out‑of‑order Emby/Jellyfin refreshes dropping content.
  • More accurate posters via release‑year matching; a loading shimmer while they fetch and a progress bar during first‑time artwork setup.

Guide & interface

  • Much lighter EPG handling — large guides no longer contend with playback, and failing sources back off instead of retrying every launch.
  • Loading indicators throughout (counts, provider lists, libraries, home) instead of blank/empty on launch.
  • Full Dynamic Type pass; proper iPad split‑view browse layout.
  • Credentials in stream URLs are now redacted from logs.

Requires macOS 14 or later. Universal (Apple Silicon + Intel).

item.title: Version 20.1
item.description:
A big stability and polish release — the headline fix is the iPhone playback freeze on the Native engine, plus a major overhaul of resume, media‑server reliability, the guide, and loading indicators.

Playback

  • Fixed the periodic video freeze on iPhone — live channels on the Native engine could freeze for ~a second every few minutes while audio kept playing. The engine also now auto‑recovers when a server drops the connection.
  • The Native engine works again over plain‑HTTP on iOS 26, restoring the higher‑quality deinterlacer for sports and interlaced channels.
  • Sheds expensive image processing when the device gets hot, so long sessions stutter less.
  • Assorted player fixes (volume‑slider drag, catch‑up timing, engine selection).

Watch progress & resume

  • Resume actually works now across movies and series, on IPTV and Emby/Plex/Jellyfin — progress is saved reliably during playback.
  • Continue Watching is sorted by most‑recent; “Start from Beginning” starts immediately.
  • Fixed progress routing with multiple media servers; Plex now reports mid‑playback.

Media servers & artwork

  • Fixed Jellyfin opening the Emby view, and out‑of‑order Emby/Jellyfin refreshes dropping content.
  • More accurate posters via release‑year matching; a loading shimmer while they fetch and a progress bar during first‑time artwork setup.

Guide & interface

  • Much lighter EPG handling — large guides no longer contend with playback, and failing sources back off instead of retrying every launch.
  • Loading indicators throughout (counts, provider lists, libraries, home) instead of blank/empty on launch.
  • Full Dynamic Type pass; proper iPad split‑view browse layout.
  • Credentials in stream URLs are now redacted from logs.

Requires macOS 14 or later. Universal (Apple Silicon + Intel).

item.title: Version 20.2
item.description:
A feature release — profiles, a big push on discovery and metadata (trailers, trending, ratings), Trakt two‑way sync, and cross‑device resume on iPhone, plus two fixes from 20.1.

Profiles

  • User profiles — each with its own watchlist, watch progress, and hidden channels/groups, while playlists, settings, and favorites stay shared. Optionally PIN‑lock a profile.

Discovery & metadata

  • Trailers now match the title's language — a foreign‑language movie/series plays its own‑language trailer instead of always the English one.
  • “Trending This Week” discovery row, resolved into titles you can actually play from your catalog.
  • Featured carousel on the iPhone Home screen; trailer button and TMDB ratings on iPhone detail pages.

Trakt, sync & remote

  • Trakt two‑way sync — watchlist changes push to Trakt, and your Trakt watched history imports back to mark items watched.
  • Cross‑device resume on iPhone over iCloud, with a newest‑wins merge; clearing an item now clears it everywhere.
  • QR pairing for the iPhone remote — scan the code on the Mac to pair instantly.

Playback & guide

  • Load an external subtitle file on macOS directly from the player (parity with iPhone).
  • Experimental audio passthrough (bitstream AC3/DTS/TrueHD to a receiver) and an experimental on‑disk SQLite guide store for very large EPGs — both off by default.

Fixes

  • Per‑episode resume for Plex series — episodes were sharing one resume time; each now tracks its own position.

Requires macOS 14 or later. Universal (Apple Silicon + Intel).

item.title: Version 20.2
item.description:
A feature release — profiles, a big push on discovery and metadata (trailers, trending, ratings), Trakt two‑way sync, and cross‑device resume on iPhone, plus two fixes from 20.1.

Profiles

  • User profiles — each with its own watchlist, watch progress, and hidden channels/groups, while playlists, settings, and favorites stay shared. Optionally PIN‑lock a profile.

Discovery & metadata

  • Trailers now match the title's language — a foreign‑language movie/series plays its own‑language trailer instead of always the English one.
  • “Trending This Week” discovery row, resolved into titles you can actually play from your catalog.
  • Featured carousel on the iPhone Home screen; trailer button and TMDB ratings on iPhone detail pages.

Trakt, sync & remote

  • Trakt two‑way sync — watchlist changes push to Trakt, and your Trakt watched history imports back to mark items watched.
  • Cross‑device resume on iPhone over iCloud, with a newest‑wins merge; clearing an item now clears it everywhere.
  • QR pairing for the iPhone remote — scan the code on the Mac to pair instantly.

Playback & guide

  • Load an external subtitle file on macOS directly from the player (parity with iPhone).
  • Experimental audio passthrough (bitstream AC3/DTS/TrueHD to a receiver) and an experimental on‑disk SQLite guide store for very large EPGs — both off by default.

Fixes

  • Per‑episode resume for Plex series — episodes were sharing one resume time; each now tracks its own position.

Requires macOS 14 or later. Universal (Apple Silicon + Intel).

item.title: Version 20.3
item.description:
A big interface release — a redesigned, more immersive TV experience with full keyboard & remote control — plus fixes on top of 20.2.

Immersive interface

  • Redesigned immersive UI on a unified design system — consistent color grammar (white focus, red LIVE), refined typography, coherent spacing.
  • Full keyboard & remote navigation of the channel grid and program guide — arrow-key movement, horizontal (time) navigation across the EPG, two-stage channel-number entry.
  • Browse while you watch — surf channels and explore the grid without interrupting playback.
  • Redesigned OSD — full-width bottom scrim with the channel number, and a cleaner zap banner showing the channel group and time remaining.
  • New Recents rail replaces “Up Next For You”.
  • Polished throughout — unified toasts, pills, and empty states; live-channel cards adopt the immersive tile look.

Settings

  • Consolidated Integrations tab — Trakt and TMDB in one place; version info now shows your update channel.

Fixes

  • Per-episode resume for Plex series now works — episodes were sharing one resume time; each now tracks its own position.
  • Correct version shown in About / Settings on development builds.

Requires macOS 14 or later. Universal (Apple Silicon + Intel).

item.title: Version 20.3
item.description:
A big interface release — a redesigned, more immersive TV experience with full keyboard & remote control — plus fixes on top of 20.2.

Immersive interface

  • Redesigned immersive UI on a unified design system — consistent color grammar (white focus, red LIVE), refined typography, coherent spacing.
  • Full keyboard & remote navigation of the channel grid and program guide — arrow-key movement, horizontal (time) navigation across the EPG, two-stage channel-number entry.
  • Browse while you watch — surf channels and explore the grid without interrupting playback.
  • Redesigned OSD — full-width bottom scrim with the channel number, and a cleaner zap banner showing the channel group and time remaining.
  • New Recents rail replaces “Up Next For You”.
  • Polished throughout — unified toasts, pills, and empty states; live-channel cards adopt the immersive tile look.

Settings

  • Consolidated Integrations tab — Trakt and TMDB in one place; version info now shows your update channel.

Fixes

  • Per-episode resume for Plex series now works — episodes were sharing one resume time; each now tracks its own position.
  • Correct version shown in About / Settings on development builds.

Requires macOS 14 or later. Universal (Apple Silicon + Intel).
